fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Delaware Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Delaware - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity delaware, how to transfer audiologist license to delaware, audiologist compact delaware, delaware audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Speech/Language Pathologists, Audiologists and Hearing Aid Dispensers
board.mailing_address: Cannon Building, 861 Silver Lake Blvd., Suite 203, Dover, DE 19904
board.physical_address: 861 Silver Lake Blvd., Suite 203, Dover, DE 19904

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Delaware.

steps.title: Register for a DELPROS Account
steps.description: Go to the DELPROS online portal, select 'Register a new User Account', and follow the prompts to create your professional profile and log in.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log into your DELPROS account, select 'Apply for a New License', choose 'Audiologist' from the list, and accurately complete all sections of the application form.

steps.title: Submit Required Documents
steps.description: Upload or arrange for direct submission of official transcripts from your university, verification of your supervised professional experience (e.g., CFY report), and official Praxis examination scores to DELPROS.

steps.title: Verify Out-of-State Licensure (if applicable)
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, request verification of current and good standing licensure from all states where you have previously held an Audiologist license to be sent directly to DELPROS.

steps.title: Pay Application Fees
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $117 directly through the DELPROS system upon submission of your complete application.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: Monitor your application status in DELPROS; once all required documentation is received and reviewed, the Board will process your application and notify you of approval for your Delaware Audiologist license.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Delaware audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Delaware have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Delaware participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Delaware without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Delaware?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Delaware while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Delaware
hero.dek: To apply for an Audiologist license in Delaware, navigate to the DELPROS online portal, create an account, and submit your application along with all required supporting documentation.
